title: D4 SSE + chokidar /events endpoint
description: Implement GET /events endpoint returning text/event-stream (Server-Sent Events). Use chokidar to watch .fabric/* and .intent-ledger.jsonl in the project root. On file change, push typed SSE events (meta-changed | ledger-appended | human-lock-changed) with incremental payload. Include connection limit, 30s heartbeat ping, and client cleanup on disconnect. Mount on createFabricHttpApp Express app from TASK-006. Also define the FabricEvent discriminated union in @fabric/shared/schemas/events.ts (already scaffolded in TASK-001).

files.target: GET /events SSE endpoint with chokidar file watcher

files.change: Import eventsRouter from api/events.ts; app.use('/events', eventsRouter); pass projectRoot to eventsRouter factory

implementation: Create packages/server/src/api/events.ts: implement createEventsRouter(projectRoot: string): Router factory; set SSE response headers; use chokidar.watch(['.fabric', '.intent-ledger.jsonl'], {cwd: projectRoot, ignoreInitial: true})

Map chokidar events to FabricEvent types: 'agents.meta.json' change → MetaChangedEvent; '.intent-ledger.jsonl' change → LedgerAppendedEvent (read last N lines as payload); 'human-lock.json' change → HumanLockChangedEvent
Implement 30s heartbeat: setInterval(() => res.write(':ping\n\n'), 30000); clear on res.on('close')
Implement max-connection guard: keep a Set<Response> of active SSE clients; reject with 503 if > 10
On req.socket.on('close'): call watcher.close(), clearInterval, remove from clients Set
Update http.ts to pass projectRoot to createEventsRouter and mount at /events

reference.examples: FabricEvent discriminated union defined in @fabric/shared/schemas/events.ts from TASK-001; chokidar is already a transitive dep or can be added to server package.json

criteria: curl -N http://127.0.0.1:7373/events maintains open connection (does not immediately close)
Externally modifying .fabric/agents.meta.json (e.g., touch) triggers 'meta-changed' SSE event within 500ms
Appending a line to .intent-ledger.jsonl triggers 'ledger-appended' SSE event within 500ms
10-minute idle connection stays alive (heartbeat pings every 30s visible in curl output)
Closing the curl connection does not leave dangling chokidar watchers (verify via process lsof)

rationale.chosen_approach: SSE is unidirectional push — sufficient for Dashboard audit view which only reads data from server; chokidar is the de-facto file-watching library in Node.js ecosystem
alternatives_considered: Long polling (inefficient, adds unnecessary HTTP overhead)
Re-using MCP SSE notifications (couples dashboard events to MCP protocol)
decision_factors: SSE is simpler to implement and consume in Preact (native EventSource API)
rationale.tradeoffs: SSE has browser max-connection-per-origin limit (6) — acceptable for single-user local dashboard with 1 SSE connection

manual_checks: curl -N http://127.0.0.1:7373/events — verify persistent connection with periodic ':ping' lines
In separate terminal: touch /path/to/project/.fabric/agents.meta.json — verify SSE event appears in curl
success_metrics: SSE event delivered within 500ms of file change
No resource leaks on client disconnect

risks.description: chokidar not available as a direct dependency (only transitive)
risks.mitigation: Add chokidar as explicit dependency in packages/server/package.json; it is already used by many build tools in the ecosystem
risks.fallback: Use fs.watch() as fallback (less reliable on macOS) or poll-based approach
